Stop $204: Confirmed — pre-breakout base + 50-day MA cluster at ~$205-207. Today's intraday wick to $203.08 is a real warning sign. Watch the June 18 close.
TP1 adjustment: Stored $240 sits just above 52-week high ($236.54) / ATH close ($235.47, May 14). Recommend adjusting TP1 to $235 (ATH resistance).
TP2 $260 / TP3 $285: Valid — analyst consensus cluster begins ~$299-310.
Intraday breach alert: Jun 18 range $203.08–$209.21, open $208.53. Opened above stop, sold off through the session. Classic institutional distribution after a retail gap-up. No daily close below $204 yet (Jun 17 close: $204.65). Pre-decide: if Jun 18 closes below $204, exit rule triggers.

Stop Flag — Needs Tightening:
Stop $915 is 17% below current ~$1,112. No structural market support exists at $915 — this stop has no basis in current price action. Real support sits at the $1,047–$1,086 range (S2-S3 pivot support). Suggest tightening stop to $1,047 (just below S3 pivot). Current $915 stop only makes sense if you want to hold through extreme volatility with no invalidation logic.
TP1 $1,114: Confirmed near analyst cluster ($1,108–$1,165 resistance). Approaching — LLY is nearly at TP1 with today's ~$1,112 estimate.
TP2 $1,243 / TP3 $1,393: Within analyst target range. Valid bull case.
Position note: LLY is your best performer (+11.75% unrealized). GLP-1 secular trend intact. At estimated ~$1,112, you are essentially AT TP1 resistance. No action today — but begin watching for a TP1 trim signal if confirmed close above $1,114.

Noted as recent FDA news.

Option A — ACHV Catalyst Play
36 shares × $5.57 = $200.52 + $3 commission = $203.52 deployed · 30% cap check: 0.30 × $823.63 = $247.09 > $203.52 ✓ within limit · BUT: PDUFA June 20 is 2 days away — pure binary event. Entry now = maximum uncertainty.
Option B — Add to AVGO
+0.53 shares × $384 = $203.52 + $3 = $206.52 · Blended avg: ~$394.43 · Problem: Adding to a position that is only $5 above its stop is averaging down into risk, not strength.
Recommendation: HOLD CASH
Two positions (NVDA and AVGO) are simultaneously under pressure — NVDA with an intraday stop breach and AVGO only $5 from its stop. Cash is your buffer right now. Deploying $200+ into a binary bet or averaging into a stressed position is the wrong move when you may need that cash to redeploy after stop-outs. Let the dust settle first.

Exception: If you run "analyze ACHV" and conviction is high, a small partial position ($50–75) could work — leaving $130+ cash buffer intact.

NVDA and AVGO near stops — exiting before thesis confirmation would lock in losses violating commission rules. Recommendation: No rotations today. Monitor and defend.
Tomorrow's Game Plan
1
Check NVDA June 18 closing price tonight
Did it close below $204? If yes: prepare to exit at market open June 19. If it closed above $204: intraday breach only — hold and watch. Pre-decide before the open.
2
Verify AVGO and LLY confirmed closing prices
Today's AVGO and LLY prices are estimates. Confirm actuals on Yahoo Finance at market close. Update mental stops accordingly.
3
Run "analyze ACHV" if interested in the June 20 PDUFA
Only 2 days to event. If you want to take a position, need to do it Friday (June 19). The full 8-section deep dive will clarify conviction and size. Don't enter blind.
4
Consider tightening LLY stop to $1,047
Current $915 stop has no structural basis. S3 pivot support at $1,047 is the logical level. This isn't urgent but protects your best gain if LLY reverses.
5
Watch TSM near TP1 at $435
Only $2.85 away. Net trim profit is thin ($2.22) — hold for TP1 break and aim for $452 (R1). If TSM prints above $452 on a close, reassess the trim size.
Concept #22 Daily Education
Pre-Market & After-Hours Liquidity Traps
Pre-market and after-hours sessions have 10–20x lower volume than regular trading hours — meaning a single institutional order can move price dramatically with no real buyers behind it. When NVDA gapped up from $204.65 (June 17 close) to $208.53 this morning, retail traders saw green and chased. What followed was textbook institutional distribution: smart money used that retail enthusiasm to unload inventory throughout the session, pushing NVDA down to a $203.08 intraday low — a $5.45 round-trip from open to trough, breaching your stop in the process. The gap-up was the trap. The session-long sell-off was the exit door for institutions.
Also today: Gap Fill
When a stock gaps up at the open but price action quickly reverses and fills back to the prior close — that's a "gap fill." NVDA opened at $208.53 (above prior close $204.65) and traded to $203.08 — not only filling the gap but going below it. Stocks that can't hold a gap within the first 30 minutes often fill completely within 1–3 days. This is a sign that the gap was driven by noise (pre-market order imbalances, news reaction) rather than sustained institutional conviction.
Practical rule: Never make entry/exit decisions based on pre-market prices alone. The opening 15 minutes of regular trading is where real supply and demand are discovered. Wait for the first 15-minute candle to close before reading direction.
